name: Build
goal: Build the skill/kypr/ skeleton: directory structure, install.py, verify_encryption.py, kypr.py, Claude Code adapter, workspace-state/ layout, and .gitattributes. All git-crypt security constraints must be enforced before any personal data paths exist.
definition_of_done: skill/kypr/ directory structure exists with all files from projections.json
verify_encryption.py exits 0 in a correctly configured repo and exits 1 with a clear error otherwise; no bypass path exists
install.py copies all projections, calls verify_encryption.py, is idempotent, enforces path containment, and skips .work/ if already present
kypr.py is agent-agnostic: --status and --workspace <name> work; calls verify_encryption.py at startup
adapters/claude-code/ contains settings.json (SessionStart hook) and startup.sh; no core logic in the adapter
workspace-state/ layout defined and documented; all personal-data paths covered by .gitattributes filter=git-crypt rules
.gitattributes exists with at least one filter=git-crypt rule covering workspace-state/ personal-data paths
am_i_done.py passes on all phase 2 feature branches
constraints: No personal data files committed before .gitattributes git-crypt rules are in place (WORK-0013 before WORK-0012 data)
verify_encryption.py must be called before any file projection or session start — not optional, not conditional
kypr.py must contain no harness-specific code; adding a second harness requires no changes outside adapters/
ReDoS fix (WORK-0014) is low priority but must not be silently dropped
assumptions_to_validate: Claude Code settings.json supports a SessionStart hook that can call an external script
git-crypt status exits 0 reliably in a fully unlocked clone (not just initialised)
projections.json flat array is sufficient for install.py without needing hedl's tier resolution logic
learning_goals: Does the single-tier projections.json model cover all projection cases without edge-case workarounds?
Is the directory-convention adapter boundary (no adapters.json) truly sufficient for multi-harness support?
What is the minimum viable workspace-state/ schema for personal/professional isolation?
